title: buildah push:  dial tcp: lookup gitea.127.0.0.1.nip.io: no such host
body:
## Issue

When the command `buildah push` is executed within a Tekton task = pod then we got as error `buildah push:  dial tcp: lookup gitea.127.0.0.1.nip.io: no such host` 

Log
```
buildah --storage-driver=overlay push \
  --tls-verify=false \
  --digestfile /tmp/image-digest gitea.127.0.0.1.nip.io:8443/giteaadmin/my-quarkus-app \
  docker://gitea.127.0.0.1.nip.io:8443/giteaadmin/my-quarkus-app

...

[buildah-image : build-and-push] ## Build the project ...
[buildah-image : build-and-push] STEP 1/11: FROM registry.access.redhat.com/ubi8/openjdk-21:1.20
[buildah-image : build-and-push] Trying to pull registry.access.redhat.com/ubi8/openjdk-21:1.20...
[buildah-image : build-and-push] Getting image source signatures
[buildah-image : build-and-push] Checking if image destination supports signatures
[buildah-image : build-and-push] Copying blob sha256:8dc97931d0a29118b7e8dd695ac355f7b569223a972f3eb87f2ff07fc9fc190a
[buildah-image : build-and-push] Copying blob sha256:b46e4e7892d6177335aee5445f59105231c351f2fb68a24f25ee7b2656e29674
[buildah-image : build-and-push] Copying config sha256:b8d81704f56858c6859ce949133635bb716162ddd3c8d012ec77572449403153
[buildah-image : build-and-push] Writing manifest to image destination
[buildah-image : build-and-push] Storing signatures
[buildah-image : build-and-push] STEP 2/11: ENV LANGUAGE='en_US:en'
[buildah-image : build-and-push] STEP 3/11: COPY --chown=185 target/quarkus-app/lib/ /deployments/lib/
[buildah-image : build-and-push] STEP 4/11: COPY --chown=185 target/quarkus-app/*.jar /deployments/
[buildah-image : build-and-push] STEP 5/11: COPY --chown=185 target/quarkus-app/app/ /deployments/app/
[buildah-image : build-and-push] STEP 6/11: COPY --chown=185 target/quarkus-app/quarkus/ /deployments/quarkus/
[buildah-image : build-and-push] STEP 7/11: EXPOSE 8080
[buildah-image : build-and-push] STEP 8/11: USER 185
[buildah-image : build-and-push] STEP 9/11: ENV JAVA_OPTS_APPEND="-Dquarkus.http.host=0.0.0.0 -Djava.util.logging.manager=org.jboss.logmanager.LogManager"
[buildah-image : build-and-push] STEP 10/11: ENV JAVA_APP_JAR="/deployments/quarkus-run.jar"
[buildah-image : build-and-push] STEP 11/11: ENTRYPOINT [ "/opt/jboss/container/java/run/run-java.sh" ]
[buildah-image : build-and-push] COMMIT gitea.127.0.0.1.nip.io:8443/giteaadmin/my-quarkus-app
[buildah-image : build-and-push] Getting image source signatures
[buildah-image : build-and-push] Copying blob sha256:dd5e77a90e609b328f2e49aa60e50bd8837e505c157060c337725413ccf449f1
[buildah-image : build-and-push] Copying blob sha256:8b30b41a0b038bf660c4538ae04bb77b7cdbfcfcb0f5a378129ddf82b91542e7
[buildah-image : build-and-push] Copying blob sha256:ec15a2d6fac8531e5afc505c6ef53bf8cac2102e47e6dac524cc6ab6256b6396
[buildah-image : build-and-push] Copying config sha256:ee491f49d22eb85a3927a35acbb52ac293916b8449d84410f17257238a6cf056
[buildah-image : build-and-push] Writing manifest to image destination
[buildah-image : build-and-push] --> ee491f49d22e
[buildah-image : build-and-push] Successfully tagged gitea.127.0.0.1.nip.io:8443/giteaadmin/my-quarkus-app:latest
[buildah-image : build-and-push] ee491f49d22eb85a3927a35acbb52ac293916b8449d84410f17257238a6cf056
[buildah-image : build-and-push] Getting image source signatures
[buildah-image : build-and-push] Copying blob sha256:ec15a2d6fac8531e5afc505c6ef53bf8cac2102e47e6dac524cc6ab6256b6396
[buildah-image : build-and-push] Copying blob sha256:dd5e77a90e609b328f2e49aa60e50bd8837e505c157060c337725413ccf449f1
[buildah-image : build-and-push] Copying blob sha256:8b30b41a0b038bf660c4538ae04bb77b7cdbfcfcb0f5a378129ddf82b91542e7
[buildah-image : build-and-push] Error: pushing image "gitea.127.0.0.1.nip.io:8443/giteaadmin/my-quarkus-app" to "docker://gitea.127.0.0.1.nip.io:8443/giteaadmin/my-quarkus-app": trying to reuse blob sha256:dd5e77a90e609b328f2e49aa60e50bd8837e505c157060c337725413ccf449f1 at destination: pinging container registry gitea.127.0.0.1.nip.io:8443: Get "https://gitea.127.0.0.1.nip.io:8443/v2/": dial tcp: lookup gitea.127.0.0.1.nip.io: no such host
```
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/994c7e20-313a-4630-81a0-52595c85f2fe)


**Note**: We can curl the same host/registry if I deploy a simple Tekton task and curl
```
[curl-gitea] ## Pinging gitea.127.0.0.1.nip.io ...
[curl-gitea]   % Total    % Received % Xferd  Average Speed   Time    Time     Time  Current
[curl-gitea]                                  Dload  Upload   Total   Spent    Left  Speed
  0     0    0     0    0     0      0      0 --:--:-- --:--:-- --:--:--     0* Host gitea.127.0.0.1.nip.io:8443 was resolved.
[curl-gitea] * IPv6: (none)
[curl-gitea] * IPv4: 10.96.186.193
[curl-gitea] *   Trying 10.96.186.193:8443...
[curl-gitea] * ALPN: curl offers h2,http/1.1
[curl-gitea] } [5 bytes data]
[curl-gitea] * TLSv1.3 (OUT), TLS handshake, Client hello (1):
```
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/dfd453d4-7546-4f45-bcf5-48f92114c465)


Buildah version: `buildah version 1.37.3 (image-spec 1.1.0, runtime-spec 1.2.0)`

comments.body:
I did a test using curl image vs podman and oberserver the same issue
## That works when pod uses image: quay.io/curl/curl
```
[curl-gitea : curl-gitea] ## Host and DNS nameservers cfg ...
[curl-gitea : curl-gitea] # Kubernetes-managed hosts file.
[curl-gitea : curl-gitea] 127.0.0.1	localhost
[curl-gitea : curl-gitea] ::1	localhost ip6-localhost ip6-loopback
[curl-gitea : curl-gitea] fe00::0	ip6-localnet
[curl-gitea : curl-gitea] fe00::0	ip6-mcastprefix
[curl-gitea : curl-gitea] fe00::1	ip6-allnodes
[curl-gitea : curl-gitea] fe00::2	ip6-allrouters
[curl-gitea : curl-gitea] 10.244.0.54	build-push-image-curl-gitea-pod
[curl-gitea : curl-gitea] search demo.svc.cluster.local svc.cluster.local cluster.local dns.podman
[curl-gitea : curl-gitea] nameserver 10.96.0.10
[curl-gitea : curl-gitea] options ndots:5

[curl-gitea : curl-gitea] ## Using as image: quay.io/curl/curl

[curl-gitea : curl-gitea] ## Curl to the registry host: https://gitea.cnoe.localtest.me:8443/api/swagger ...
[curl-gitea : curl-gitea]   % Total    % Received % Xferd  Average Speed   Time    Time     Time  Current
[curl-gitea : curl-gitea]                                  Dload  Upload   Total   Spent    Left  Speed
  0     0    0     0    0     0      0      0 --:--:-- --:--:-- --:--:--     0* Host gitea.cnoe.localtest.me:8443 was resolved.
[curl-gitea : curl-gitea] * IPv6: (none)
[curl-gitea : curl-gitea] * IPv4: 10.96.91.49
[curl-gitea : curl-gitea] *   Trying 10.96.91.49:8443...
[curl-gitea : curl-gitea] * ALPN: curl offers h2,http/1.1
[curl-gitea : curl-gitea] } [5 bytes data]
...
[curl-gitea : curl-gitea] * using HTTP/2
[curl-gitea : curl-gitea] * [HTTP/2] [1] OPENED stream for https://gitea.cnoe.localtest.me:8443/api/swagger
[curl-gitea : curl-gitea] * [HTTP/2] [1] [:method: GET]
[curl-gitea : curl-gitea] * [HTTP/2] [1] [:scheme: https]
[curl-gitea : curl-gitea] * [HTTP/2] [1] [:authority: gitea.cnoe.localtest.me:8443]
[curl-gitea : curl-gitea] * [HTTP/2] [1] [:path: /api/swagger]
[curl-gitea : curl-gitea] * [HTTP/2] [1] [user-agent: curl/8.10.1]
[curl-gitea : curl-gitea] * [HTTP/2] [1] [accept: */*]
[curl-gitea : curl-gitea] } [5 bytes data]
[curl-gitea : curl-gitea] > GET /api/swagger HTTP/2
[curl-gitea : curl-gitea] > Host: gitea.cnoe.localtest.me:8443
[curl-gitea : curl-gitea] > User-Agent: curl/8.10.1
[curl-gitea : curl-gitea] > Accept: */*
```
## Fails using as image [quay.io/containers/podman](http://quay.io/containers/podman) or [quay.io/containers/buildah](http://quay.io/containers/buildah)
```
[create-container-remote-docker : create-container] Creating a container against daemon host: 10.89.0.1.
[create-container-remote-docker : create-container] ## Host and DNS nameservers cfg ...
[create-container-remote-docker : create-container] # Kubernetes-managed hosts file.
[create-container-remote-docker : create-container] 127.0.0.1	localhost
[create-container-remote-docker : create-container] ::1	localhost ip6-localhost ip6-loopback
[create-container-remote-docker : create-container] fe00::0	ip6-localnet
[create-container-remote-docker : create-container] fe00::0	ip6-mcastprefix
[create-container-remote-docker : create-container] fe00::1	ip6-allnodes
[create-container-remote-docker : create-container] fe00::2	ip6-allrouters
[create-container-remote-docker : create-container] 10.244.0.55	build-push-image-create-container-remote-docker-pod
[create-container-remote-docker : create-container] search demo.svc.cluster.local svc.cluster.local cluster.local dns.podman
[create-container-remote-docker : create-container] nameserver 10.96.0.10
[create-container-remote-docker : create-container] options ndots:5

[create-container-remote-docker : create-container] ## Using as image: quay.io/containers/podman:v5.0.3

[create-container-remote-docker : create-container] ## Curl to the gitea registry at: gitea.cnoe.localtest.me:8443
[create-container-remote-docker : create-container]   % Total    % Received % Xferd  Average Speed   Time    Time     Time  Current
[create-container-remote-docker : create-container]                                  Dload  Upload   Total   Spent    Left  Speed
  0     0    0     0    0     0      0      0 --:--:-- --:--:-- --:--:--     0* Could not resolve host: gitea.cnoe.localtest.me
[create-container-remote-docker : create-container] * Closing connection
[create-container-remote-docker : create-container] curl: (6) Could not resolve host: gitea.cnoe.localtest.me
```

Please troubleshoot the task and the cluster - in the log which illustrates a problem resolving "gitea.127.0.0.1.nip.io", it appears to already have successfully resolved and communicated with "registry.access.redhat.com".

> Please troubleshoot the task and the cluster - in the log which illustrates a problem resolving "gitea.127.0.0.1.nip.io"

No more information is reported. Something is preventing to access an address exposed by the nginx server on the kubernetes cluster. As you can see we can curl using the curl image but not using podman or buildah images

So, when we use the image `quay.io/curl/curl` running as Tekton task = pod and when we do a curl command then the following hostname `gitea.cnoe.localtest.me` is resolved against the kubernetes IP address of the `ingress-nginx` service where such a host is exposed using an ingress resource

Example
```
curl -v gitea.cnoe.localtest.me

[curl-gitea : curl-gitea] ## Using as image: quay.io/curl/curl
[curl-gitea : curl-gitea] ## Curl to the registry host: https://gitea.cnoe.localtest.me:8443/api/swagger ...

[curl-gitea : curl-gitea]   % Total    % Received % Xferd  Average Speed   Time    Time     Time  Current
[curl-gitea : curl-gitea]                                  Dload  Upload   Total   Spent    Left  Speed
  0     0    0     0    0     0      0      0 --:--:-- --:--:-- --:--:--     0* Host gitea.cnoe.localtest.me:8443 was resolved.

[curl-gitea : curl-gitea] * IPv6: (none)
[curl-gitea : curl-gitea] * IPv4: 10.96.128.185
[curl-gitea : curl-gitea] *   Trying 10.96.128.185:8443...
[curl-gitea : curl-gitea] * ALPN: curl offers h2,http/1.1
...
```
The IP address `10.96.128.185` corresponds to the IP address of the service `ingress-nginx` which manages the ingress resources like `gitea.cnoe.localtest.me`
```
ingress-nginx                ingress-nginx-controller                  NodePort    10.96.128.185   <none>        8443:30442/TCP,80:32131/TCP,443:31879/TCP   24m
```

**Question**: Why such a resolution `gitea.cnoe.localtest.me` -> `10.96.128.185` is not taking place when we use the `buildah` or `podman` image which uses the same DNS nameservers as curl
```
cat /etc/resolv.conf
search demo.svc.cluster.local svc.cluster.local cluster.local dns.podman
nameserver 10.96.0.10
```

I'm becoming mad as all the tests that I'm doing work using: ping, curl, dig executed part of a pod created using dnsutils tool : kubectl apply -f https://k8s.io/examples/admin/dns/dnsutils.yaml work but not buildah

## 0. Test buildah push (NOK)

`
[buildah-image : build-and-push] Getting image source signatures
[buildah-image : build-and-push] Copying blob sha256:edd3472621b6b7febb667fe1cdb3a4421fc4e04a2165596f84ee3b948dabdcf5
[buildah-image : build-and-push] Copying blob sha256:dd5e77a90e609b328f2e49aa60e50bd8837e505c157060c337725413ccf449f1
[buildah-image : build-and-push] Copying blob sha256:8b30b41a0b038bf660c4538ae04bb77b7cdbfcfcb0f5a378129ddf82b91542e7
[buildah-image : build-and-push] Error: pushing image "gitea.cnoe.localtest.me:8443/giteaadmin/my-quarkus-app" to "docker://gitea.cnoe.localtest.me:8443/giteaadmin/my-quarkus-app": trying to reuse blob sha256:dd5e77a90e609b328f2e49aa60e50bd8837e505c157060c337725413ccf449f1 at destination: pinging container registry gitea.cnoe.localtest.me:8443: Get "http://gitea.cnoe.localtest.me:8443/v2/": dial tcp: lookup gitea.cnoe.localtest.me: no such host
`

## 1. Test using DIG (OK)

### CoreDNS log
```
[INFO] 10.244.0.34:48924 - 3815 "A IN gitea.cnoe.localtest.me. udp 64 false 4096" NOERROR qr,aa,rd 113 0.000078125s
```
### DIG
```

kubectl exec -i -t dnsutils -- dig gitea.cnoe.localtest.me
E1007 13:59:17.052919   16272 websocket.go:296] Unknown stream id 1, discarding message

; <<>> DiG 9.16.27 <<>> gitea.cnoe.localtest.me
;; global options: +cmd
;; Got answer:
;; ->>HEADER<<- opcode: QUERY, status: NOERROR, id: 3815
;; flags: qr aa rd; QUERY: 1, ANSWER: 1, AUTHORITY: 0, ADDITIONAL: 1
;; WARNING: recursion requested but not available

;; OPT PSEUDOSECTION:
; EDNS: version: 0, flags:; udp: 4096
; COOKIE: 7b2fa01f5e43ef58 (echoed)
;; QUESTION SECTION:
;gitea.cnoe.localtest.me. IN  A

;; ANSWER SECTION:
ingress-nginx-controller.ingress-nginx.svc.cluster.local. 6 IN A 10.96.14.241

;; Query time: 1 msec
;; SERVER: 10.96.0.10#53(10.96.0.10)
;; WHEN: Mon Oct 07 11:59:17 UTC 2024
;; MSG SIZE  rcvd: 136
```

### CoreDNS
```
[INFO] 10.244.0.34:52236 - 58946 "AAAA IN gitea.cnoe.localtest.me.default.svc.cluster.local. udp 67 false 512" NOERROR qr,aa,rd 160 0.000073625s                                                          │
[INFO] 10.244.0.34:52236 - 58738 "A IN gitea.cnoe.localtest.me.default.svc.cluster.local. udp 67 false 512" NOERROR qr,aa,rd 139 0.000102542s                                                             │
```

### Curl
```
kubectl exec -i -t dnsutils -- curl -s -k  https://gitea.cnoe.localtest.me:8443
*   Trying 10.96.14.241:8443...

* ALPN, offering h2
* ALPN, offering http/1.1
* successfully set certificate verify locations:

* TLSv1.3 (IN), TLS handshake, CERT verify (15):
* TLSv1.3 (IN), TLS handshake, Finished (20):
* TLSv1.3 (OUT), TLS change cipher, Change cipher spec (1):
* TLSv1.3 (OUT), TLS handshake, Finished (20):
* SSL connection using TLSv1.3 / TLS_AES_256_GCM_SHA384
* ALPN, server accepted to use h2
* Server certificate:
*  subject: O=cnoe.io

*  issuer: O=cnoe.io
*  SSL certificate verify result: self signed certificate (18), continuing anyway.
* Using HTTP2, server supports multiplexing
* Connection state changed (HTTP/2 confirmed)
* Copying HTTP/2 data in stream buffer to connection buffer after upgrade: len=0
* Using Stream ID: 1 (easy handle 0xaaaaeb64fca0)
> GET / HTTP/2
> Host: gitea.cnoe.localtest.me:8443
...
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en-US" data-theme="gitea-auto">
<head>
  <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1">
  <title>Gitea: Git with a cup of tea</title>
```

## 3. Test using ping

###  CoreDNS
```
[INFO] 10.244.0.34:37510 - 43524 "AAAA IN gitea.cnoe.localtest.me.default.svc.cluster.local. udp 67 false 512" NOERROR qr,aa,rd 160 0.000067583s                                                          │
[INFO] 10.244.0.34:37510 - 43358 "A IN gitea.cnoe.localtest.me.default.svc.cluster.local. udp 67 false 512" NOERROR qr,aa,rd 139 0.000082042s
```

### Ping (OK)
```
kubectl exec -i -t dnsutils -- ping -c 4 gitea.cnoe.localtest.me
E1007 14:03:58.078530   20398 websocket.go:296] Unknown stream id 1, discarding message
PING gitea.cnoe.localtest.me (10.96.14.241): 56 data bytes
64 bytes from 10.96.14.241: seq=0 ttl=62 time=0.199 ms
64 bytes from 10.96.14.241: seq=1 ttl=62 time=0.568 ms
64 bytes from 10.96.14.241: seq=2 ttl=62 time=0.812 ms
64 bytes from 10.96.14.241: seq=3 ttl=62 time=0.449 ms
```
The problem I reported here has nothing to do with buildah, podman images but due to a wrong coreDNS rewrite rule
